text:
Kurapika's Memories: Part Two (クラピカ②, Kurapika Tsuioku-hen (Ni)) is the second chapter of a two-part one-shot of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Synopsis

Upon reaching the bustling Nancha City, Kurapika is ecstatic to see all the people and things around him. Pairo reminds him that he can't get too excited, and Kurapika quickly calms down. Pairo points out that they have 17 and a half hours left, and Kurapika is excited to get all their shopping done, explaining that they will be able to get everything they need at something called a department store. The two eventually find the large building, and Pairo happily pulls out a jar full of coins, revealing that he saved all the tips from his shopping chores and leaving Kurapika impressed. Once inside the store, Pairo suggests splitting up since it will be faster, and although Kurapika worries about him being on his own, Pairo quickly tells him to trust his partner more.

With a full shopping cart, Kurapika notices several customers staring at him. He looks at his reflection in a mirror to make sure his eyes aren't scarlet, wondering if it seems strange for a kid to be buying so many things. He then suddenly hears a loud noise and finds Pairo knocked to the floor by three thugs, one of whom angrily tells Pairo to watch where he's going. Kurapika hurriedly checks on him, but Pairo assures him that he just tripped. The main thug points out that he has ice cream all over his clothes, wondering what the two plan to do about it. An old man appears and calls the thugs liars, beginning to say that they bumped into the boy, but another thug cuts him off and threatens him with violence, causing the man to shrink back. Pairo apologizes and mentions his poor eyesight, but the main thug replies that instead of an apology, he wants money so he can get the stain out.

Kurapika sternly asks how much he wants, but the thug rebukes him and asks how much they have. Pairo explains that they are running errands and holds out the jar of coins, saying that it's all they have of their own money. Kurapika is against his decision, but Pairo assures him that it's okay. The thug snatches the jar, and all three laugh when they see that all the coins are one jennies. The thug tells Pairo that his change won't pay for anything as he pours it all onto the floor. With Kurapika becoming even more upset, Pairo tells him to stop and says that it was all his fault, offering to apologize and bowing down to the thug. Kurapika quickly follows, and both loudly apologize to the thugs. The bystanders begin to side with the two boys and tell the thugs to leave them alone. The three quickly leave the store, and a few of the bystanders help the two boys pick up the remaining coins. An old woman wonders if they are on a trip and says that the three thugs are well known in the town. She repeatedly apologizes for not stepping in sooner, but the two thank them for their help.

After leaving the store with their three Piko (two of which are carrying supplies), Kurapika says that they were able to finish their shopping so quickly because of everyone's help. He reminds Pairo that Dino Hunter said there will always be people willing to help no matter what. Kurapika suggests checking out the town since they still have time, but Pairo disagrees and says that they should head back to the village, explaining that they should leave early since the weather could change on the way back. As Kurapika agrees, the two suddenly spot the three thugs standing on the path in front of them. Kurapika suggests ignoring them, and Pairo agrees, but one of the thugs stops them and says that they have to pay the toll or they won't be allowed to pass. Kurapika suggests going around the farm in the distance, but the main thug menacingly explains that they need to pay them to leave the town. Kurapika replies that he's never heard of such a law, and the thug calls him ignorant. Kurapika then points out that they don't have any more money, and the thug tells him to leave their stuff behind.

Pairo suddenly pulls out a cell phone, and the thug offers to let them pass if he gives it to them. Pairo then reveals that he's calling the police to ask them if they really have to pay a toll, leaving the thug speechless. Multiple townspeople begin to approach the group and wonder what they're doing, causing the main thug to nervously tell the two boys to get going. Kurapika sticks out his tongue at him, but as Pairo tries to pass, the thug trips him and makes him collapse on the ground. Kurapika quickly checks on him, but Pairo assures him that he's fine. The thug acts innocent and stresses that he didn't do anything, and Pairo says that he fell on his own, smiling at Kurapika and adding that they should get going. As the two walk away, the thug tells Kurapika that he feels bad for him since he has to take care of a gimp, adding that it must not be much fun. Kurapika's eyes widen, and he suddenly leaps toward the thug before punching him in the face.

With one of them pulling out a knife, the two other thugs prepare to attack Kurapika, but he quickly subdues them and leaves them yelling out in pain on the ground. His eyes now scarlet, Kurapika screams at them to get up before kicking the main thug in the midsection. The thugs desperately apologize and ask for forgiveness, but Kurapika coldly replies that he doesn't want an apology, telling them to die. Pairo tells him to stop, and as the two argue with each other, the main thug reveals that they were hired to confront them. Kurapika asks for more details, and he explains that a few days earlier an old man agreed to pay them if they could get him angry. Kurapika asks if his hair was like a horn, and the thug confirms that he also had a beard and a scar on his forehead. Kurapika then asks if the old man told him to say what he said about Pairo. The thug nervously confirms that he did, but Pairo says that he's lying since it was only decided half a day ago that he would go on the trip and the old man couldn't have asked them to confront them a few days ago.

As Kurapika prepares to attack him, the thug desperately apologizes and says that the old man told them they would be with an adult. He adds that the deal was to get the adult angry and that the old man said the two already had an agreement. He explains that the old man said the plans had changed and that he would only pay them half, again apologizing and adding that what he said about Pairo was on a whim. As Kurapika angrily says that it's no excuse, Pairo gives the cell phone to the thug and tells him to call the old man to explain that he couldn't get them angry. The thug immediately does so and also tells the old man that he doesn't need to pay them. He again apologizes to the two boys and says that he got carried away, bowing down and asking for forgiveness. Pairo says that they should get going, but Kurapika suddenly feels a small rock hit him on the back of the head, looking back to see a small crowd of townspeople. Among them, the old woman from the department store prepares to throw another rock, telling Kurapika to get away from the three boys and angrily calling the two red-eyed freaks. She throws the rock and orders them to leave the town, calling them the devil's messengers. A young woman begs her grandmother to stop and fearfully yells that the two will kill them all if they get any angrier. Another rock hits Kurapika as Pairo leads him away and the two head back to the village.

During the return trip, Pairo explains that when the Kurta get scarlet eyes, especially when anger is involved, they lose reason and gain incredible strength. He adds that it's what frightens everyone and that hiding their red eyes isn't enough. Kurapika says that he forgot about how sunglasses and contact lenses aren't enough. He then playfully asks Pairo if he was impressive back in the town, noting how easily he knocked out the thugs. However, he wonders if they really don't belong in the outside world, but Pairo tells him that he needs to pass the exam so he can go and see it, adding that the encounter they had was a good lesson.

Kurapika points out that he failed since his eyes turned scarlet, realizing that the phone call was for nothing. However, Pairo hands him a small mirror, and Kurapika is shocked to see that his eyes are somehow back to normal even after the eyedrops. Pairo happily reveals that he switched the bottles, explaining that he dropped it on purpose and had them use his eye medicine instead. He then tells Kurapika not to think that he didn't trust him, adding that he overheard the Elder talking to the thug on the phone. After the two return to the village, the Elder tells Kurapika that he has passed the exam and will be allowed to leave for an indefinite amount of time.

The others are surprised by the indefinite aspect and wonder if it's too much. The Elder leads Kurapika away from the others and hands him an envelope, explaining that he wrote detailed notes about Pairo's eyes and legs. He tells Kurapika to search as much as he likes, leaving him taken aback, and stresses that he actually doesn't want to let him go to the outside world. On the day of departure, Kurapika is seen riding a Piko as his parents tell him to be careful and that a short temper won't be of any use. Pairo then tells him to take care of himself, and Kurapika promises to find a doctor who can heal him, asking him to pass the exam too so that they can go on a trip together. Pairo promises to pass the exam and adds that while he's happy Kurapika wants to find a doctor for him, the reason they want to see the outside world is because they wanted an enjoyable adventure like the one in Dino Hunter.

Taken aback at first, Kurapika quickly smiles and says that he's right. Pairo tells Kurapika that when he returns, he will ask him if he had fun. He asks him to promise to have a trip where he can answer "yes" from the bottom of his heart. Kurapika happily promises and then rides off as Pairo and the other members wave goodbye. As Kurapika looks back at Pairo with a giant smile on his face, it's stated that the Kurta Clan massacre was made known to the world six weeks later. A detailed description of the report is then given.

A female traveler who had wandered into the forest found the bodies of all 128 villagers. Family members sat facing each other, stabbed multiple times and their heads severed while they were still alive. Those who were pure Kurta Clan members had both their eyes gouged out, while those who had married into the clan had their eyes squashed, sustaining more vicious wounds than the other members. The adult males had more wounds, but it's assumed that these were sustained during a struggle before they were captured by the perpetrators. It's believed that those who had married into the clan were mutilated to set an example. Those whose eyes turned scarlet were then decapitated one after another. The eyes' scarlet color is highly valued in the underworld, and it's believed that the perpetrators deliberately mutilated the children in front of their parents to produce a more vibrant scarlet color. Lastly, a message believed to be from the perpetrators was found near the bodies: "We'll accept anything you leave, but don't ever take anything away from us."

Characters in Order of Appearance
